steps.caption_md: Scanning `s = "([])"`. Index 0 is an opener, **(**, so push it. Stack: `[]` → `['(']`.
Index 1 is another opener, **[**. Push it too — the stack now remembers both opens, most recent on top.
Index 2 is a closer, **]**. Before doing anything, what does the top of the stack tell us has to close first?

predict.prompt_md: At index 2 we see closer **]** and the stack is `['(', '[']`. What happens next?

choices.label_md: Pop the stack — the top is `[`, which matches `]` — and continue.
Push `]` onto the stack like any other token.
Return false immediately because the stack has more than one item.

predict.explanation_md: The top of the stack is always the most recently opened, still-unmatched bracket — the only thing a closer can legally match. Here `[` matches `]`, so we pop and continue; if the top were `(` instead, the string would be invalid.

steps.caption_md: Top of stack was **[**, which matches **]** — pop it. Stack: `['(']`.
Index 3 is **)**; the top of the stack is **(**, a match — pop it. The stack is now empty and the string is fully consumed: valid.
